mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in less than 50% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5%, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and rising recession probability, causing S&P 500 forward returns to turn negative over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses above the 5.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal income growth combined with stable consumer price inflation indicates healthy demand without monetary policy tightening pressure, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 60% of occurrences or the interaction term loses statistical significance at p > 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth during periods of low monetary policy rates signals strong supply-side momentum that translates into immediate positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses or becomes statistically indistinguishable from zero when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and rising corporate revenues, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 55% of months with positive payroll growth over a 5-year rolling validation window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in housing starts exceeding 5% signals a sharp contraction in construction demand and household wealth effects, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in more than 70% of instances following a housing starts decline greater than 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation occurring alongside a flat or rising federal funds rate indicates restrictive monetary conditions that compress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative returns when inflation growth exceeds 3% annually, or the interaction effect is insignificant in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth during periods of stable unemployment confirms sustainable economic expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative in more than 60% of quarters where GDP growth is positive but unemployment rises concurrently by more than 0.2 percentage points.
